workflow.name: Send AI lead follow-up SMS with GPT-4.1, Google Sheets, Twilio and Gmail
workflow.description:
## How it works
- Triggered when a lead submits your website contact form via webhook
- Logs the lead's Name, Phone, Email, and Interest to Google Sheets
- GPT-4.1 generates a personalised SMS and sends it instantly via Twilio
- Waits 2 hours, then checks if the lead has replied (via Google Sheets)
- If replied: notifies you via Gmail. If not: sends an AI follow-up SMS

## Set up steps
- Add your OpenAI API key to both OpenAI nodes (model: GPT-4.1)
- Connect your Twilio account (Account SID + Auth Token) in both SMS nodes
- Link Google Sheets via OAuth and set your sheet with columns: Name, Phone, Email, Interest, Replied
- Connect Gmail via OAuth for owner notifications
- Paste the webhook URL into your website contact form as the POST endpoint
- Manually update the `Replied` column to `Yes` in Google Sheets when a lead responds

workflow.name: Speed To Lead + Reply Handling V1

parameters.responseBody: Thank you! We have received your enquiry and will be in touch shortly.

values.content:
=You are a friendly, professional real estate assistant. 

A new lead has just submitted an enquiry form. Your job is to write a warm, personalized SMS message that gets sent to them instantly.

Here are the lead's details:
- Name: {{ $('Webhook - Lead Form Trigger').item.json.body.name }}
- Property Type they are interested in: {{ $('Webhook - Lead Form Trigger').item.json.body.interest }}

Rules you must follow:
1. Keep the message under 160 characters if possible, never exceed 320 characters
2. Address the lead by their first name only
3. Mention their specific property type and location so it feels personal
4. End with exactly one question to encourage a reply
5. Sound like a real human texting from their phone — warm, casual, not corporate
6. Never use emojis excessively — one at most
7. Never use words like "greetings", "dear", or "valued customer"
8. Sign off with the agent's first name only

Output only the SMS message text. Nothing else. No labels, no explanations, no quotation marks.

=You are a friendly, professional real estate assistant.

A lead submitted an enquiry 48 hours ago and has not replied to the first message. Your job is to write a gentle, non-pushy follow-up SMS to re-engage them.

Here are the lead's details:
- Name:{{ $('Webhook - Lead Form Trigger').item.json.body.name }}
- Property Type they are interested in: {{ $('Webhook - Lead Form Trigger').item.json.body.interest }}

Rules you must follow:
1. Keep the message under 320 characters
2. Acknowledge that you are following up without making them feel guilty for not replying
3. Briefly mention their property interest to keep it personal
4. Offer a simple low-commitment action — like replying YES for a quick call
5. Sound human and warm, not automated
6. Never say "this is a follow-up message" or "as per my previous message"
7. Sign off with the agent's first name only

Output only the SMS message text. Nothing else. No labels, no explanations, no quotation marks.

parameters.content: ## Check the reply status and take next steps accordingly

# 🤖 AI Lead Follow-Up Automation

Automatically respond to website enquiries, log leads, send AI-personalised SMS messages, and follow up — all without lifting a finger.

---

## ⚙️ How It Works

1. A lead submits your **website contact form**, triggering the webhook
2. The lead is **logged to Google Sheets** (`Name`, `Phone`, `Email`, `Interest`)
3. **GPT-4.1** generates a personalised initial SMS based on the lead's details
4. **Twilio** sends the first SMS to the lead
5. The workflow **waits 2 hours**
6. **Google Sheets** is checked — has the `Replied` column been marked `Yes`?
7. **If YES** → A **Gmail notification** is sent to you (the agent/owner)
8. **If NO** → **GPT-4.1** generates a follow-up SMS and **Twilio** sends it

---

## 🛠️ Setup Checklist

**Webhook**
- [ ] Copy the webhook URL and paste it as the POST endpoint in your website contact form
- [ ] Ensure your form sends: `name`, `phone`, `email`, `interest`

**Google Sheets**
- [ ] Create a sheet with columns: `Name` | `Phone` | `Email` | `Interest` | `Replied`
- [ ] Connect your Google account in the Google Sheets nodes
- [ ] Update the sheet ID in both Google Sheets nodes to match your sheet
- [ ] Manually update the `Replied` column to `Yes` when a lead responds

**OpenAI**
- [ ] Add your **OpenAI API key** in both OpenAI nodes
- [ ] Model is set to `gpt-4.1` — change if needed
- [ ] Customise the system prompts in both nodes to match your business tone and offer

**Twilio**
- [ ] Add your **Twilio Account SID** and **Auth Token** in both Twilio nodes
- [ ] Set your **Twilio phone number** as the sender in both SMS nodes
- [ ] Ensure the `phone` field from your form includes the country code (e.g. `+91...`)

**Gmail**
- [ ] Connect your **Gmail account** in the Gmail node
- [ ] Update the recipient email to your own address for lead reply notifications

---

## ⚠️ Important Notes

> The `Replied` status is **manually updated** in Google Sheets. For a fully automated reply-detection system, consider integrating a Twilio inbound webhook.

> The **2-hour wait** uses n8n's built-in Wait node. Adjust the duration based on your follow-up strategy.

> Make sure your n8n instance is on a **paid or self-hosted plan** to support long waits and webhook triggers in production.

---

## 🔑 API Keys Required

| Service | Where to Get It |
|---|---|
| OpenAI | [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com) |
| Twilio | [console.twilio.com](https://console.twilio.com) |
| Google Sheets |   Via Google OAuth in n8n credentials |
| Gmail | Via Google OAuth in n8n credentials |

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
